framing: U.S. Army Signal Corps officer transitioning to cybersecurity analyst role at defense contractor

jd.title: Cybersecurity Analyst III — Defensive Cyber Operations
jd.style_note: Defense/cleared: clearance required, specific certifications, government contract language

requirements.text: Active TS/SCI clearance with CI polygraph
5+ years of experience in cybersecurity operations, incident response, or threat analysis
DoD 8140 compliant certification (CASP+, CISSP, or CCNP Security)
Experience with SIEM platforms (Splunk, ArcSight, or Elastic)
Knowledge of MITRE ATT&CK framework and adversary TTPs
Experience with host-based and network-based intrusion detection systems
Familiarity with NIST 800-53 and RMF (Risk Management Framework) processes
Experience supporting DoD or IC networks
Scripting ability in Python, PowerShell, or Bash for automation
Experience with cloud security (AWS GovCloud or Azure Government)
BS in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related field (or equivalent military training)

responsibilities: Monitor and defend DoD networks against advanced persistent threats (APTs)
Conduct incident response and forensic analysis on compromised systems
Develop and tune SIEM detection rules and correlation logic
Produce threat intelligence reports and briefings for government stakeholders
Support RMF authorization packages and continuous monitoring requirements
Collaborate with red team and vulnerability assessment teams

expected_output.summary: U.S. Army Signal Corps Captain (separating) with 7 years of military cybersecurity experience including 3 years leading a Cyber Protection Team (CPT) at U.S. Cyber Command. TS/SCI with CI polygraph (active). CompTIA CASP+ certified. Hands-on experience with incident response, SIEM operations (Splunk), and network defense on classified DoD networks. BS Information Technology from West Point.

experience.title: Cyber Protection Team Leader (CPT-12)
bullets: Led 15-person Cyber Protection Team conducting defensive cyber operations on DoD Information Networks (DoDIN)
Directed incident response for 25+ cyber incidents including APT intrusions on classified networks
Managed Splunk SIEM deployment monitoring 50K+ endpoints, authoring 40+ custom detection rules
Produced weekly threat intelligence briefings for brigade-level commanders using MITRE ATT&CK framework
Achieved 98% compliance on RMF continuous monitoring requirements across 3 authorization boundaries

experience.company: U.S. Army — 1st Signal Brigade, Camp Humphreys, South Korea
experience.title: Signal Officer / Platoon Leader
bullets: Managed tactical communications and network security for 800-person infantry battalion
Implemented NIST 800-53 security controls during NIPR/SIPR network consolidation project
Led team of 22 signal soldiers maintaining satellite, radio, and network infrastructure
Completed Army Cyber School (16 weeks) and earned CompTIA CASP+ certification

education: BS Computer Science — United States Military Academy at West Point (2014-2018)

jd_requirements: 5+ years cybersecurity incident response threat analysis
DoD 8140 compliant CASP+ CISSP CCNP Security
cloud security AWS GovCloud Azure Government
BS Computer Science Cybersecurity military training
deliberate_flaws: No CISSP (has CASP+ which meets DoD 8570 but CISSP is industry gold standard)
No Python scripting — only PowerShell and Linux CLI
No cloud security experience (AWS GovCloud or Azure Government)
Military jargon throughout — needs civilian translation for resume
No ArcSight or Elastic experience (only Splunk)
Forensic analysis is basic (EnCase exposure, not certified EnCE)
BS is Computer Science — strong match, but no graduate degree

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
